items.acceptanceCriteria: equipment documentation is available
Previous inspection reports are checked
Daily pre-operational inspection checklists are checked (if applicable)
Preventive Maintenance is established and updated records are maintained
items.referenceStandard: ASME B30.5 Sec:5-2.3.1 ASME B 30.8 Sec :8-2.3.1

items.acceptanceCriteria: Operator is certified (for the inspected crane type/model) and licensed
Operator manuals are available
A sign is posted warning the operator not to rely solely on any automatic device as a substitute for safe operating practice
Crane is painted safety yellow
Crane is painted safety yellow-and-black stripes on offshore facilities
Rated capacity of crane is marked (Arabic & English)
Means are provided for the operator to visually determine machine list and machine trim.
A durable rating chart with legible letters and figures is provided and fixed at a location visible to the operator while seated at his control station
Load ratings at all operating radii and boom angles, and for all permissible boom lengths, jib lengths, and angles are available
Recommended parts of hoist reeving, size, and type of rope for various loads are shown either on the rating chart or in the operating manual
Essential precautionary or warning notes relative to limitations on equipment, operating procedures, and stability factors are provided on the rating chart or the operating manual
Structure integrity and capability are verified to withstand the proposed or actual duty
Protective guards are in place and Secured at areas of potential hazard
Navigational lights are provided
Flame arrester on fill and of gasoline tank is provided
Fuel tank is equipped with self-closing filler cap
Vertical clearance between crane lowest point and floor level of boat or barge is at least 7 feet(if less than 7 feet, barracading is to be provided to protect personnel from rotating Sections)
All controls are within the reach of operator and clearly marked with functions and modes of operation
Remote control of the crane is properly working and crane stops if control signal becomes ineffective (if fitted)
Electrical motors disconnect on power failure
Electrical motors do not start on power return
Over-speeding preventing device is provided for electric motor operated cranes
Power plant controls are provided to • Control start, stop and lock in off position • Control engine speed • Stop diesel engines in emergency, and • Shift selective transmissions
Controls’ forces are: • Hand levers not greater than 35 lb (156 N) • Foot pedals not greater than 50 lb (222 N) or less than 8 lb (36 N) on foot pedals
Clutch is operating properly and within the reach of operator station
Resistors and connectors are corrosion free, protected, ventilated and installed to prevent the accumulation of combustible matter near hot parts
Resistor units are supported and do not vibrate
Cab is provided with safety glazing glass
Cab door swings out to open or slides rearward for sliding door type (door operation is restrained during machine operation)
Cab wipers are in good condition and operating
A clear passageway is provided from operator's position to outside the cab
Platform to cab walkway is skid resistance
Handholds/steps are provided to facilitate access to the cab

Communication method is one of the following: • Hand signals (standard chart is posted in visible location to the operator) • Radio/telephone • Bell signal • Special signals (to be understood and agreed upon by operator and signalperson)
Wire rope clips attached with U-bolts are having the U-bolt over the dead end of the rope and the live rope resting in the clip saddle
The rope does not have more than 6 randomly distributed broken wires in 1 lay or 3 in 1 strand (for running ropes)
The rope does not have broken outer wire at the contact point with the rope core (for running ropes)
The rope wear does not exceed 1/3 of the original diameter of outside individual wires
The rope does not have kinking, crushing, birdcaging, evidence of heat damage, ubstranding, core corrosion, main strand displacement or any other damages
The reduction does not exceed 5%
The ropes does not have more than two broken wires in 1 lay in sections beyond end connections or more than 1 broken wire at an end connection (for standing ropes)
Wire rope is not corroded
Rope lubrication is adequate
Sheaves and drums are not cracked or show worn surfaces
Bolts and rivets around the structure are tight
Clutch system parts, linings, pawls and ratchet are not excessively worn
Brake system parts are not excessively worn
Rope reeving is in compliance with manufacturer's recommendations
Sheave sizes are not less than 15 times the nominal rope diameter for boom hoist
sheave sizes are not less than 18 times the nominal rope diameter for load hoist
Load block sheaves shall have pitch diameters not less than 16 times the nominal diameter of the rope used
Eye splices are in accordance with the manufacturer's recommendations and rope thimbles are used
Boom hoist drum is provided with an auxiliary a locking device controllable from the operator’s station to hold the drum from rotating in the lowering direction and to hold the rated load
Boom hoist drum has at least 2 full wraps remaining on the drum when the boom is at lowest position
The load hoist mechanism is provided with a suitable clutching or power engaging device, unless directly coupled
Over hoist limit devices are operable (when provided)
All welded members and joints have no cracks, distortion, corrosion or other defects
Boom stops are provided and in good condition (one of the following; fixed or telescopic bumper, shock absorbing bumber, hydraulic boom elevation cylinder, or derrick masts)
Boom angle indicator is provided and readable from operator's cab
Boom hoist disconnect, shutoff, or hydraulic relief is provided to stop the boom hoist automatically when the boom reaches a predetermined angle
Cords and lacings have no damage
Guy ropes are correctly tensioned
Derrick mast fittings and connections are in compliance with the manufacturer's recommendations
Clutch system parts, linings, pawls and ratchet are not excessively worn
Operational Aids are equipped and functioning
Chain drive sprockets do not show excessive wear and is not stretched
Gudgeon pins do not have cracks, wear or distortion
Supports do not have any defects and have continued ability to sustain the imposed loads
Hydraulic and pneumatic hoses, fittings and tubes are not damaged and not leaking
Hydraulic/Pneumatic pumps and motors are correctly functioning
Hydraulic/Pneumatic pumps and motors have no leaks, excess vibrations, unusual noises, loss of pressure or loss of speed
Hydraulic/Pneumatic valves and motors are correctly functioning
Hydraulic/Pneumatic valves and motors have no leaks, corrosion, excess vibrations, unusual noises or loss of pressure
Hydraulic/ Pneumatic cylinders do not leak (external and internal) at seals and welded joints or any other locations
Hydraulic/ Pneumatic cylinders are free of scores, dents or nicks on piston rods or cylinder barrels and loose/deformed rod eyes or connecting joints
Hydraulic filters are clean and free from debris such as rubber or metal particles
Boom cradle is available and can secure the boom if required
Pins, bearings, shafts, gears, rollers, and locking devices are free of wear, cracks, and distortion
The swing mechanism is controlling the swing of the rated load under all operating conditions
Swing braking operates and capable of restrict the movement of the rotating structure
Swing brake locking device is provided and capable of locking the rotation of rotating structure
Swing brakes are adjustable to compensate for wear
All swing moving parts are lubricated
Exhaust pipes are insulated and gases are properly discharged away from the operator's cab
A portable fire extinguishers are in place inside cab and outside machinery (minimum rating of 10 BC)
A tool box with basic maintenance tools is placed inside the cab
An audible warning device is in place and within reach of the operator
The load moment indicator (LMI) is installed and properly working (if 2720 kg or more)
The cab and operating enclosures have good housekeeping
The machinery and electrical equipment are located clear of deck loading areas
Work areas, companion ways and ladders are equipped with anti-slip surface materials
Electrical wiring and equipment are free of damages and of the specified type for shipboard
Manholes and hatches are not less than 15" x 22" (380 mm x 560 mm) and raised above the deck to prevent accidental entry of spilled liquids
Labeling and manufacturer data are available and legible
Safe working load is clearly marked on the hook
Hook is not bent or twisted
Hook is not distorted in the throat opening • Max. allowable throat opening is 5% not to exceed 1∕4 in (6mm) or as per manufacturer recommendations
Maximum wear in the hook bowl is not exceeding 10% (compared to new hook) or as per manufacturer recommendations
Hook is not cracked, gouged or shows nicks
Hook can lock (if it is a self-locking hook)
Inoperative latch (if provided) — any damaged latch or malfunctioning latch that does not close the hook’s throat
